issues.detail: solid/prefer-for: Array#map used instead of <For /> for thinkingOptions list. Pre-existing issue — AppearanceTab.tsx was not in the HEAD~5 diff.

5 tests fail because the vi.mock for @tauri-apps/api/core does not export isTauri, but rust-bridge.ts imports it. isTauri was already present in rust-bridge before the recent change window (HEAD~5). Pre-existing breakage.
2 tests fail — same root cause as tauri-agent (missing isTauri in mock). Pre-existing.
3 tests fail: useRustTools, useRustAgent, useRustMemory — environment misconfigurations (URL parsing failures, WebSocket mock issues). Pre-existing.
6 tests fail — E2E real-scenario tests require a live backend (Tauri/Rust). Pre-existing; not caused by recent changes.

Early return before hooks: isDelegationTool check returns <SubagentCard> before calling hooks (useSettings, createSignal). This violates the rules-of-hooks convention — hooks must not be called conditionally. The hook calls that follow are only reached for non-delegation tools, which means SolidJS's reactive graph may be inconsistent. Low-severity since the component currently works, but it is architecturally fragile.

ImagesBlock and TimestampLine defined as inline functions inside the component body. These close over reactive props and are called as JSX tags (not <Dynamic>). In SolidJS this is safe as long as the parent component re-evaluates them, but they are not true components (no reactive tracking boundary). Minor: no bug, but idiomatic SolidJS would use const ImagesBlock: Component = () => ... at module scope or extract them.

debugLog called at component top level (outside JSX/createEffect) reads reactive props.thinking and displayMode(). This runs once at mount only; later signal changes will not re-trigger the log. Not a bug, but a reactivity tracking miss.
No new console.log/warn/debug calls introduced in the HEAD~5 diff. All console usage in src/ is isolated to logger.ts, dev-console.ts, and env-bridge files.

onCopy prop passed as empty arrow function () => {} to MessageBubble. MessageBubble.onCopy prop is typed as () => void and MessageActions calls it after clipboard write — so the no-op is intentional (the actions component handles copy internally). Not dead code per se, but the MessageRow does not expose onCopy to callers, meaning any copy telemetry at the MessageList level is silently dropped.

summary: TypeScript compiles cleanly (0 errors). The single ESLint error (solid/prefer-for in AppearanceTab.tsx) and all 18 failing tests are pre-existing issues predating the HEAD~5 change window — none were introduced by the recent diff. The 15 recently changed TS/TSX files show no new anti-patterns beyond minor issues: an early-return-before-hooks in ToolCallCard (fragile but functional), an inline component definition pattern in MessageBubble (idiomatic deviation), and a top-level debugLog in ThinkingRow that won't react to signal changes. No new console.log/warn calls. No broken imports. New components (TurnDiffSummary, CompactionDivider) are clean SolidJS. Overall the frontend is in good shape.
